My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json
import pandas as pd

business_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:10']
print('Loaded data, type:', type(business_data))
print('Length:', len(business_data))

# Create DataFrame
df_business = pd.DataFrame(business_data)
print('Columns:', list(df_business.columns))
print('Shape:', df_business.shape)

# Check review_count data type
print('\nReview count type:', df_business['review_count'].dtype)
print('First few review_counts:', list(df_business['review_count'][:5]))

# Check if review_count needs conversion
print('\nConverting review_count to numeric...')
df_business['review_count'] = pd.to_numeric(df_business['review_count'], errors='coerce')
print('Converted types:', df_business['review_count'].dtype)

# Now extract state from description
print('\nExtracting states from descriptions...')
state_pattern = r'(\b[A-Z]{2}\b)'  # Match 2-letter uppercase state codes

def extract_state(description):
    if pd.isna(description):
        return None
    # Find all potential state codes
    matches = re.findall(state_pattern, description)
    common_states = {'AL', 'AK', 'AZ', 'AR', 'CA', 'CO', 'CT', 'DE', 'FL', 'GA', 
                     'HI', 'ID', 'IL', 'IN', 'IA', 'KS', 'KY', 'LA', 'ME', 'MD',
                     'MA', 'MI', 'MN', 'MS', 'MO', 'MT', 'NE', 'NV', 'NH', 'NJ',
                     'NM', 'NY', 'NC', 'ND', 'OH', 'OK', 'OR', 'PA', 'RI', 'SC',
                     'SD', 'TN', 'TX', 'UT', 'VT', 'VA', 'WA', 'WV', 'WI', 'WY',
                     'DC'}
    
    for match in matches:
        if match in common_states:
            return match
    return None

import re
df_business['state'] = df_business['description'].apply(extract_state)

print('\nStates extracted:')
print(df_business['state'].value_counts().head(10))

# Calculate total reviews per state
state_reviews = df_business.groupby('state')['review_count'].sum().sort_values(ascending=False)
print('\nTotal reviews by state:')
print(state_reviews.head(10))

# Save to JSON for next steps
result_json = state_reviews.head(10).to_json()
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result_json)

import re

print('Shape:', df_business.shape)

df_business['review_count'] = pd.to_numeric(df_business['review_count'], errors='coerce')
print('Review count stats:', df_business['review_count'].describe())

# Extract state from description using two-letter state code pattern
state_pattern = r'(\b[A-Z]{2}\b)'
COMMON_STATES = {'AL', 'AK', 'AZ', 'AR', 'CA', 'CO', 'CT', 'DE', 'FL', 'GA',

import json, pandas as pd, re

# Load business data
business_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:34']
print('Business records loaded:', len(business_data))

# Load review aggregation data (review count and avg rating per business_ref)
review_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:32']
print('Review records loaded:', len(review_data))

# Create DataFrames
df_business = pd.DataFrame(business_data)
df_reviews = pd.DataFrame(review_data)

# Print sample data
print('\nBusiness data sample:')
print(df_business.head())
print('\nReview data sample:')

args.code:
import json, pandas as pd

biz = locals()['var_functions.query_db:34']
rev = locals()['var_functions.query_db:32']

df_biz = pd.DataFrame(biz)
df_rev = pd.DataFrame(rev)

states = ['AL', 'AK', 'AZ', 'AR', 'CA', 'CO', 'CT', 'DE', 'FL', 'GA', 'HI', 'ID', 'IL', 'IN', 'IA', 'KS', 'KY', 'LA', 'ME', 'MD', 'MA', 'MI', 'MN', 'MS', 'MO', 'MT', 'NE', 'NV', 'NH', 'NJ', 'NM', 'NY', 'NC', 'ND', 'OH', 'OK', 'OR', 'PA', 'RI', 'SC', 'SD', 'TN', 'TX', 'UT', 'VT', 'VA', 'WA', 'WV', 'WI', 'WY']

def get_state(desc):
    if pd.isna(desc):
        return None
    for s in states:
        if s in desc:
            return s
    return None

df_biz['state'] = df_biz['description'].apply(get_state)
df_rev['rev_count'] = pd.to_numeric(df_rev['review_count'])
df_rev['avg_rate'] = pd.to_numeric(df_rev['avg_rating'])

df_biz['biz_num'] = df_biz['business_id'].str.replace('businessid_', '').astype(int)
df_rev['biz_num'] = df_rev['business_ref'].str.replace('businessref_', '').astype(int)

df = pd.merge(df_biz, df_rev, on='biz_num', how='inner')

state_data = df.groupby('state').agg({'rev_count': 'sum', 'avg_rate': 'mean'}).round(2).sort_values('rev_count', ascending=False)

top = state_data.iloc[0]
result = {
    'state': state_data.index[0],
    'total_reviews': int(top['rev_count']),
    'avg_rating': float(top['avg_rate'])
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

I was never fully happy with my lash extensions previously but gave them another shot for the fills and stopped going to lash fills because it was a waste of money for me. 

This last experience with the breeze esthetician's had me almost in tears this morning with the result. I've had various facials done at my doctor's facilities: chemical peels, micro laser peels, etc. They always warned me of redness and whatever downtime. However, the esthetician I went to apparently went too deep with the dermaplane scalpel. She warned of some possibility bumps and to stay hydrated, not to wear make up, wash my sheets and I did everything as she said to.

This is definitely not an acceptable result and I plan on never going to breeze again. I hope this is a warning for anyone who has considered going there.
Just moved to the area and found this blow dry bar.  The gal that did my hair had great skill and was nice enough to make a few colorist recommendations in the area. Good experience and will be back.

Breeze was great! I was going to a wedding and wanted to do a little something different for a change.  I got online and made an appointment, very simple and easy process.  Arrived a few minutes before my appointment the space is beautiful!  No wait to go to the chair.  They have the most comfortable hair wash chairs I've ever sat in.  I had Alexandra and she did a fantastic job with my hair.  Since the wedding was later that day she made the curls tighter as they would fall during the day.  My hair looked just as great from leaving the salon until the end of the night!!  A few people at the wedding said I looked like a supermodel woo hoo!! Left feeling that way!
